1990_08_28-India-Mumbai-Understanding_Of_Srimati_Radharani-CC_Adi_1_5_And_4_50_And_4_56_60-st2-vr02

Understanding Of Srimati Radharani

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Understanding Of Srimati Radharani

Event Date

28 Aug 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - Cc class

Transcriber

Lalitha Harini

Completed

31 Oct 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-074-Understanding Of Srimati Radharani (Mumbai)-CC_Madhya_1_4_6-dt_90_08_28.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

CC Adi 1.5, CC Adi 4.50, CC Adi 4.56,4.57,4.58,4.59,4.60

Tags – Places

Gokula, Vrindavan

Tags – Pastimes

Divine lilas of Sri Radha and Krishna, Krishna’s Potencies

Tags – Persons

Radha, Krishna, Gopis, Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur, , Swarupa Damodara Goswami, Narottama das Thakur, Srila Jiva Gosvami, Six Goswamis

Tags – Themes

Separation, Humility, Bhakti, Potencies, Tapasya, Prema

[Sri Radharani, the beloved of Madhava and most worshipable of the cowherd girls of Gokula Mandal. Decorated by the dress of Lord Damodara’s increasing ecstasy, you are the lady of the house of Lord Hari and the groves of Vrindavan. From the ocean of Vrishabhanu has arisen a new moon which is overwhelming the qualities of Lalita and Vishakha. O Goddess, your qualities are described by Sanaka and Sanathana Rishis and by Sanatana Goswami. Please bestow your mercy upon me.] [Translation of Radhe Jaya Jaya Madhava Dayite Song]

radha Krishna-pranaya-vikṛtir hladini Saktir asmad
ekatmanav api bhuvi pura deha-bhedaṁ gatau tau
caitanyakhyaṁ prakaṭam adhuna tad-dvayaṁ caikyam aptaṁ
radha-bhava-dyuti-suvalitaṁ naumi Krishna-svarūpam

[Cc. Adi 1.5]

[The loving affairs of Śrī Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are transcendental manifestations of the Lord’s internal pleasure-giving potency. Although Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are one in Their identity, previously They separated Themselves. Now these two transcendental identities have again united, in the form of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya. I bow down to Him, who has manifested Himself with the sentiment and complexion of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī although He is Kṛṣṇa Himself.]

‘The loving affairs of Śrī Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are transcendental manifestations of the Lord’s internal pleasure-giving potency. Although Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are one in their identity, they separated themselves eternally. Now these two transcendental identities have again united, in the form of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya. I bow down to him, who has manifested himself with the sentiment and complexion of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī although he is Kṛṣṇa himself.’

ataeva sei bhava aṅgikara kari’
sadhilena nija vaṣcha gauraṅga-Sri-hari

[Cc. Adi 4.50]

[Therefore Lord Gaurāṅga, who is Śrī Hari Himself, accepted the sentiments of Rādhā and thus fulfilled His own desires.]

‘Therefore Lord Gaurāṅga, who is Śrī Krsna himself, accepted the sentiments of Rādhā and thus fulfilled his own desires.’

Purport

Purport by His Divine Grace, AC Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada - Of the four kinds of reciprocation of loving service — dāsya, sakhya, vātsalya and mādhurya — mādhurya is considered the fullest. But the conjugal relationship is further divided into two varieties, namely svakīya and parakīya. Svakīya is the relationship with Kṛṣṇa as a formally married husband, and parakīya is the relationship with Kṛṣṇa as a paramour. Expert analysts have decided that the transcendental ecstasy of the parakīya mellow is better because it is more enthusiastic. This phase of conjugal love is found in those who have surrendered to the Lord in intense love, knowing well that such illicit love with a paramour is not morally approved in society. The risks involved in such love of Godhead make this emotion superior to the relationship in which such risk is not involved. The validity of such risk, however, is possible only in the transcendental realm. Svakīya and parakīya conjugal love of Godhead have no existence in the material world, and parakīya is not exhibited anywhere in Vaikuṇṭha, but only in the portion of Goloka Vṛndāvana known as Vraja.

Some devotees think that Kṛṣṇa is eternally the enjoyer in Goloka Vṛndāvana but only sometimes comes to the platform of Vraja to enjoy parakīya-rasa. The six Gosvāmīs of Vṛndāvana, however, have explained that Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes in Vraja are eternal, like His other activities in Goloka Vṛndāvana. Vraja is a confidential part of Goloka Vṛndāvana. Kṛṣṇa exhibited His Vraja pastimes on the surface of this world, and similar pastimes are eternally exhibited in Vraja in Goloka Vṛndāvana, where parakīya-rasa is ever existent.

In the third chapter of this epic, Śrīla Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja Gosvāmī has explicitly accepted the fact that Kṛṣṇa appears in this material world at the end of the Dvāpara age of the twenty-eighth catur-yuga of Vaivasvata Manu and brings with Him His Vrajadhāma, which is the eternal abode of His highest pastimes. As the Lord appears by His own internal potency, so He also brings all His paraphernalia by the same internal potency, without extraneous help. It is further stated here in the Caitanya-caritāmṛta that the parakīya sentiment exists only in that transcendental realm and nowhere else. This highest form of ecstasy can exist only in the most confidential part of the transcendental world, but by the causeless mercy of the Lord we can have a peep into that invisible Vraja.

The transcendental mellow relished by the gopīs in Vraja is superexcellently featured in Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī. Mature assimilation of the transcendental humor of conjugal love is represented by Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, whose feelings are incomprehensible even to the Lord Himself. The intensity of Her loving service is the highest form of ecstasy. No one can surpass Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī in relishing the qualities of the Lord through this supreme transcendental mellow. Therefore the Lord Himself agreed to assume the position of Rādhārāṇī in the form of Lord Śrī Gaurāṅga. He then relished the highest position of parakīya-rasa, as exhibited in the transcendental abode of Vraja.

rādhā-kṛṣṇa eka ātmā, dui deha dhari’
anyonye vilase rasa āsvādana kari’

[Cc. Adi 4.56]

[Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are one and the same, but They have assumed two bodies. Thus They enjoy each other, tasting the mellows of love.]

Purport

The two transcendentalists Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are a puzzle to materialists. The above description of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa from the diary of Śrīla Svarūpa Dāmodara Gosvāmī is a condensed explanation, but one needs great spiritual insight to understand the mystery of these two personalities. One is enjoying in two. Śrī Kṛṣṇa is the potent factor, and Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī is the internal potency. According to Vedānta philosophy, there is no difference between the potent and the potency; they are identical. We cannot differentiate between one and the other, any more than we can separate fire from heat.

Everything in the Absolute is inconceivable in relative existence. Therefore in relative cognizance it is very difficult to assimilate this truth of the oneness between the potent and the potency. The philosophy of inconceivable oneness and difference propounded by Lord Caitanya is the only source of understanding for such intricacies of transcendence.

In fact, Rādhārāṇī is the internal potency of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, and She eternally intensifies the pleasure of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Impersonalists cannot understand this without the help of a mahā-bhāgavata devotee. The very name “Rādhā” suggests that Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī is eternally the topmost mistress of the comforts of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. As such, She is the medium transmitting the living entities’ service to Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Devotees in Vṛndāvana therefore seek the mercy of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī in order to be recognized as loving servitors of Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu personally approaches the fallen conditioned souls of the iron age to deliver the highest principle of transcendental relationships with the Lord. The activities of Lord Caitanya are primarily in the role of the pleasure-giving portion of His internal potency.

The absolute Personality of Godhead, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, is the omnipotent form of transcendental existence, knowledge and bliss in full. His internal potency is exhibited first as sat, or existence — or, in other words, as the portion that expands the existence function of the Lord. When the same potency displays full knowledge, it is called cit, or samvit, which expands the transcendental forms of the Lord. Finally, when the same potency plays as a pleasure-giving medium it is known as hlādinī, or the transcendental blissful potency. Thus the Lord manifests His internal potency in three transcendental divisions.

sei dui eka ebe caitanya gosāṣi
rasa āsvādite doṅhe hailā eka-ṭhāṅi

[Cc. Adi 4.57]

[Now, to enjoy rasa, They have appeared in one body as Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu.]

ithi lāgi’ āge kari tāra vivaraṇa
yāhā haite haya gaurera mahimā-kathana

[Cc. Adi 4.58]

[Therefore I shall first delineate the position of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. From that description the glory of Lord Caitanya will be known.]

rādhikā hayena kṛṣṇera praṇaya-vikāra
svarūpa-śakti — ‘hlādinī’ nāma yāṅhāra

[Cc. Adi 4.59]

[Śrīmatī Rādhikā is the transformation of Kṛṣṇa’s love. She is His internal energy called hlādinī.]

hlādinī karāya kṛṣṇe ānandāsvādana
hlādinīra dvārā kare bhaktera poṣaṇa

[Cc. Adi 4.60]

[That hlādinī energy gives Kṛṣṇa pleasure and nourishes His devotees.]

Purport

Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī has elaborately discussed the hlādinī potency in his Prīti-sandarbha. He says that the Vedas clearly state, “Only devotional service can lead one to the Personality of Godhead. Only devotional service can help a devotee meet the Supreme Lord face to face. The Supreme Personality of Godhead is attracted by devotional service, and as such the ultimate supremacy of Vedic knowledge rests in knowing the science of devotional service.”

What is the particular attraction that makes the Supreme Lord enthusiastic to accept devotional service, and what is the nature of such service? The Vedic scriptures inform us that the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Absolute Truth, is self-sufficient, and that māyā, nescience, can never influence Him at all. Therefore the potency that overcomes the Supreme must be purely spiritual. Such a potency cannot be anything of the material manifestation. The bliss enjoyed by the Supreme Personality of Godhead cannot be of material composition, like the impersonalist conception of the bliss of Brahman. Devotional service is reciprocation between two, and therefore it cannot be located simply within one’s self. Therefore the bliss of self-realization, brahmānanda, cannot be equated with devotional service.

In Bhagavat Sandarbha, Srila Jiva Goswami explains the potencies of the Lord as follows. The transcendental potency of the supreme personality of Godhead by which he maintains His existance is called Sandhini. The transcendental potency by which He knows himself and causes others to, to know Him is called samvit. The transcendental potency, which He possesses transcendental bliss and causes His devotees to have bliss is called hladini.

[0:13:30 indistinct] celebrating the auspicious appearance of Srimati Radharani, Sri Radhastami. On this day, it is important that we understand according to the shastra exactly who Srimati Radharani actually is otherwise we find that most people, they chanted Radhe and they have some sentimental conception of Radha Krishna’s lila but they have no grasp or concept of the spiritual nature of the divine lila of Radha and Krishna. This is called sahajiya. Sahajiya means to cheaply think that you can enter into the divine lila of Radha and Krishna, or to just believe it’s beautiful stories that we can just sing and dance about all of the time.

Of course, there is some purification to that process. But if we want to actually understand these top most spiritual subject matters, we cannot approach them with our own sentimental feelings but we must first understand through the scriptures and through the acharyas of what is the spiritual gravity of the divine lila of Sri Sri Radha and Krishna. In fact, Radha and Krishna are unapproachable by anyone who has any material attachments within their life. The divine pastimes of Sri Sri Radha and Krishna are the enactments of the pure spiritual bliss of the spiritual world. Therefore, we must approach the pastimes of Radha Krishna, not cheaply, we must approach them through the process of vaidhi bhakti, which means through following the strict principles of the acharyas by controlling our senses, by engaging in the humble service of the spiritual master and the sadhus and by learning from them gradually, what is spiritual life. We must first understand the difference between the body and the soul. And then we must understand the various…um…features in which Krishna appears in this world. Krishna says, “janma karma ca me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ.”[Bg. 4.9] We must understand the transcendental nature of Krishna’s appearance and activities in this world. And how do we understand? We must understand this subject. Krishna explains,

tad viddhi praṇipātena
paripraśnena sevayā
upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ
jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ

[Bg. 4.34]

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

‘If you want to understand the truth, you must approach a guru. You must inquire submissively and you must render service to the spiritual master. In this way, the spiritual master will reveal knowledge unto you because he has seen the truth.’

Radharani is hladini shakti

So when we are discussing the divine lila of Radha and Krishna, we are discussing the ultimate the summum bonum or the Absolute Truth. We are discussing the highest knowledge even within the realm of transcendental knowledge. So, therefore, we must approach according to how the great acharyas are teaching us. And in this way, we can get…gain actual realization of the divine mercy of Srimati Radharani and Lord Sri Krishna. So here it is being described that Krishna is the Param Brahman. Arjuna has said, “paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ paramaṁ bhavan” [Bg. 10.12]

paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ idam uttamam. Ah...prat…that, “Krishna, You are the Supreme Brahman, You are the supreme purifier, you are the abode in which everyone is residing.” Everything was within you and you are within everything. Krishna is sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam, he is the cause of all causes. And it is being described here by Swarupa Damodara Goswami that the one Supreme Absolute Truth for the sake of eternally enjoying divine pleasure, expands himself into two forms, Radha and Krishna. Radharani is considered the hladini shakti. Hladini means the pleasure giving potency. This means that there is no difference between Radha and Krishna. Some people think that Radharani is the greatest devotee. But that in…of itself is very inaccurate. Radharani is Krishna. She is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. All the potencies and glories of Krishna are similarly within the personality of Srimati Radharani.

One separated as two

But, although they are one, they have separated themselves into two for the purpose of ecstatic, loving reciprocation. Because in order for there to be pleasure of love, there must be a lover and a beloved. Therefore, Krishna is the supreme enjoyer and Srimati Radharani is the supreme enjoyed. Krishna is the supreme um…master and Srimati Radharani is the supreme lover of that Supreme Master. Therefore bhakti or devotional service is the divine property of Srimati Radharani alone and it is only through her mercy through her grace that anyone can approach Krishna. Because as Krishna says, bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya aham evaṁ-vidho ’rjuna [Bg. 11.54]

“It is only by pure devotion that I can be approached. It is by no other means”. Try to understand that the loving affairs of Radha Krishna, there is nothing material. How is it possible? After all, great brahmacharies and sanyasis, throughout history have given up all material pleasures; have given up their families; have given up their wealth; have performed severe tapasya simply for a glimpse of Brahman or the realization of this spiritual existence of the lord. We see even great kings like Bharat Maharaj, how they renounced their kingdom. And they became ah…very, very simple living in the forest performing great tapasya simply to try to understand Brahman or the spiritual existence of the lord. So here we find Krishna who is Param Brahma, the Supreme Brahman, do you think he will be attracted to any material pleasure? This is very crazy assumption.

Therefore, the pleasure of Sri Krishna is on a purely spiritual divine platform. And therefore, we must understand the relationship of Radha Krishna, as the ultimate expression of love of God for himself. And as the aspiring devotees who are trying to gain entrance into the loving service of the lord, we must approach very, very carefully and very much according to the authorized instructions of the great acharyas.

Types of sadhana bhakti: vaidhi and raga bhakti

This evening we will celebrate Radhastami. This evening we will have a nice festival, we will describe the nature of the appearance of Radharani. And we will also discuss [clears throat] um…the nature of the land of Vrindavan. Which is the, Radharani is Vrindavaneswari, she is the queen and controller of Vrindavan. But this morning for just a few minutes, I believe it is most important, far more important than hearing the pastimes of Radha Krishna, to understand the proper approach to Radha and Krishna. Some people, they go to Vrindavan and they just Jaya Radhe Jaya Krishna, and they think they will reach Krishna in this way. This is not the process. Krishna is not so cheap. You're talking about the highest realization of Godhead, millions of times higher than realization of the Brahma Jyoti. We must understand that there are various levels of bhakti. Sadhana bhakti is divided into two parts: vaidhi bhakti and raga bhakti. Vaidhi bhakti means you do not have love of God and if you think you do, you're completely insane. Love of God means you can…you cannot have love of God and even a tinge of particle of material attachment in your heart.

Real love of God

Real love of God is only when we become completely purified from the bodily conception of life. So, if we are not on that platform, we must understand that we are in the platform of vaidhi bhakti. That means the process of devotional service through regulative principles. That means you must accept a spiritual master and you must be disciplined by him. You must be disciplined by following the regulative principles and by performing tapasya under his instruction. Krishna says in Gita, “rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś caran [Bg. 2.64].” That by following the regulative principles of freedom one can obtain the complete mercy of the lord. Um…Now, after becoming completely purified [break] bhakti one attains the state which is called raga bhakti, which means spontaneous some spontaneous enthusiasm to serve Radha and Krishna, where there is no more any material desires to impede our devotion to the lord. This is a very elevated stage of consciousness, it is that stage where paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate, you are simply not attracted to any of the temporary manifestations of the world. You are simply attracted to the service of Radha and Krishna. By practicing devotional service on this platform of raga bhakti for some time, then only can you enter into the realm of prema bhakti or pure love of God. So, as we emphasize very strongly, especially here in India, we cannot take this subject matter cheaply, we must approach it as a great science if we want the real results. Some people we find, they dress themselves up as gopis or Srimati Radharani and think by…by playing the role of gopis, then we will develop the sentiment of the gopis. But nowhere in the shastras and nowhere in the teachings of the bonafide acharyas is this ever accepted. In fact, the real acharyas reject this conception. You cannot simply pretend to be a gopi. First you have to ex…the gopis are the highest position of pure love. How can you pretend to be on that platform?, either you are a gopi or you are not a gopi. Therefore, a sane person who is properly following the real system, he becomes the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant of the gopis of Vrindavan.

Humility is the only way to approach God

Which means, he learns how to properly control his senses and engage all of his activities in the mission of his spiritual master. He takes the humblest position. After all, you can only approach God through utter humility. Does humility mean pretending you're on the ultimate position or to accept the lowest position? Which one? Therefore, to pretend that we are gopis transgresses the principle of humility. We are trying to play the role of the ultimate devotees of the lord before we can even control our senses properly. Even those who have attained mukti, who are totally liberated from our material desires, they have no access to the feelings of the gopis. You can only attain the feelings of the gopis through utter surrender to the feet of the lord. When you achieve the blessings in the mercy of the lord and his servants, then can we enter into these spiritual feelings not by our own endeavours. So therefore, we should know that if we truly and really want to gain the blessings of Srimati Radharani then we must take the most insignificant position of being the menial servant of the servant of the servant.

In fact, the six Goswamis, they were the majaris of Vrindavan who descended into this world. No where do we find the six Goswamis saying, “Oh, I saw Krishna. Oh, I am seeing Krishna. Yes, Krishna was dancing for me. Krishna was playing his flute for me.” The six goswamis are always praying with tears in their eyes, “Where is Radha? Where is Krishna?” Do you think that seeing Radha and Krishna is so cheap, that they just appear to you? The price of seeing Radha and Krishna is that you must be immersed in separation and longing for them to such an extent that separation totally, totally pervades your heart and you cannot think of anything else. Therefore, those who are actually on the path according to the great acharyas rather than talking about how I have seen Radha and Krishna, they are trying to cultivate the intense and genuine feelings of separation from Radha and Krishna. It is only then that Radha and Krishna actually reveal themselves to you. You see the pastimes of Radha and Krishna are not simply a subject for entertainment. Most people in this land of India, they come to pravachan more to be entertained than they do to be purified.

Therefore, when Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur was giving Srimad-Bhagavatam class for thirty days in Vrindavan. Everyone was thinking ‘He is the highest saint there is. How much nectar we will receive by hearing Krishna and Radharani lila!’ And he simply spoke for the thirty days on the first verse. Explaining the difference between matter and spirit. And most of the people went away because they were disappointed, he is not giving us the nectar. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati, [clears throat] he very purposely did this. But um…you...until you…until your senses are purified you cannot taste the nectar. Until your senses are completely purified, Radha Krishna’s lila is just a story that's completely way over your head. It's like describing PhD mathematics to a kindergardener. They might, it might sound very nice, but you have no conception of what it is. Therefore, to hear the divine lila of Radha and Krishna certainly purifying, but it must be very much in conjunction with the basic principles of Krishna consciousness. [clears throat] Therefore, we find that Srila Prabhupada, he is translating for the world, these very, very high subjects of the verses of the Sri Caitanya-caritamrta.

But he's also giving elaborate purports which are describing the exact position and condition of our consciousness, and how to elevate it. So therefore, to actually be the real servants of Srimati Radharani and to really attain her divine blessings, we must become the humble servants of the servants of the servants of the servant. If we really and truly want to gain her divine blessings, we must approach her through the via medium of the recognized sadhus and the spiritual master. And gradually step by step by step, they teach us how to become free from ahankar, false ego. Even the great devotee Narottama das Thakur, he was living in Vrindavan. He was constantly exhibiting the symptoms of ecstatic love. But how did he pray?

viṣaya chāṛiyā kabe śuddha ha'be mana
kabe hāma herabo śrī-bṛndābana

[Lalasamayi Prarthana 3]

These are the greatest acharyas how they pray, they are not saying, “Oh, I saw Krishna in dream last night. Oh, Krishna danced for me, oh Krishna.” The great acharyas they are praying; they are longing; their eyes are filled with tears. Their hearts are just, just like fountains of emotion and what is their prayer? “When or when will I be free from all material desires? Only then will I see Vrindavan only when can I…can I even approach the divine Lila of Radha and Krishna.”

Prayer to Srimati Radharani on Radhastami day

he rādhe! vraja-devike! ca lalite! he nanda-suno! kutaḥ [Ṣaḍ-gosvāmy-aṣṭaka 8] and the six Goswamis they are praying, “Where is Radharani? Where are you, Krishna? Are you at Govardhan? Are you at Vamsi Vat? My heart is simply longing in separation for you.” So, we must cultivate these feelings of separation. We must cultivate the feeling of yearning and longing for the divine service of the divine couple Sri Sri Radha and Krishna. And these divine feelings of separation, they awake within our heart only by the mercy of Srimati Radharani. And that mercy is coming through her representatives.

And therefore, on this holy day of Radhastami, we should be very much um…sober and at the same time joyful too. On the order of Guru, assisting in his divine service of giving pleasure to Sri Sri Radha Gopinath. On this day, of course, we can pray to Srimati Radharani, she is the giver of devotion. She is the giver of love. Our prayer should be that, “Srimati Radharani, oh beloved of Sri Krishna!, by your causeless and infinite mercy, by your kindness kind me…kindly allow me to gradually become purified by engaging as the servant of the servant of the servant of your devoted servants.” There is no prayer that is more pleasing to Srimati Radharani than this and if we offer this prayer, with a deep and sincere desire to actually live according to this prayer. That is the greatest gift; that is the greatest offering we can offer on this auspicious day of Radhastami. So, everyone please come this evening we'll have a nice celebration the bhajans and kirtans will begin at four o'clock. Hare Krishna!
